summary: Nine concrete items surfaced from discussion-rich front-page threads.
telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 20/20 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Leanstral 1.5
items.summary: Mistral's reasoning model tuned for proof-heavy, small-model tasks.
items.why_included: Commenters said it stays useful on OCR and file-analysis workloads without frontier-model pricing.
items.evidence: One commenter said Mistral is great for OCR and file analysis at tiny cost, and another suggested trying Leanstral 1.5 through OpenATP.

items.name: OpenATP
items.summary: An open-source theorem-prover runner with Docker and Modal support.
items.why_included: It is the concrete tool commenters pointed to for trying Leanstral 1.5.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote "Try out Leanstral 1.5 on the latest version of OpenATP" and linked the repo and docs.

items.name: btop
items.source_story_title: Explanation of everything you can see in htop/top on Linux (2019)
items.summary: A modern terminal system monitor with GPU, disk, network, and watt stats.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters called it a better htop replacement.
items.evidence: One user said they migrated to btop because it is modern and informative, and another said it even replaced iStatMenu on a MacBook.

items.name: BareMetal RAM Dumper
items.source_story_title: BareMetal RAM Dumper – Bare-metal x86 tool for Cold Boot Attack experiments
items.summary: A bare-metal x86 tool for cold-boot memory-dump experiments.
items.why_included: The thread showed a real hardware test, not just a concept demo.
items.evidence: The author described freezing RAM, swapping drives, booting the dumper, and successfully dumping raw physical memory.

items.name: Windows CE Dreamcast Community Edition (wince-dc)
items.summary: A Windows CE port for Dreamcast with a one-command build path.
items.why_included: Commenters highlighted how little setup it needs compared with older Windows CE workflows.
items.evidence: A commenter said one cmake invocation goes from source to a bootable disc image with no Platform Builder, SDK install, or CD key.

items.name: Anna's Archive
items.source_story_title: Google Books (or similar) all book scans – $200k bounty (2025)
items.summary: A free ebook and scan search site for hard-to-find books.
items.why_included: Readers said it kept access to books alive in markets with poor availability.
items.evidence: A commenter said they would not have read the books that shaped them without Anna's Archive and Z-Library.

items.name: send.djazz.se
items.source_story_title: Google Books (or similar) all book scans – $200k bounty (2025)
items.summary: A browser-based EPUB transfer service for Kobo readers.
items.why_included: It was explicitly called out as the key Kobo transfer trick.
items.evidence: One commenter said send.djazz.se is key for getting EPUBs to a Kobo.

items.name: Moon+ Reader
items.source_story_title: Google Books (or similar) all book scans – $200k bounty (2025)
items.summary: A mobile ebook reader with library, annotation, and file tools.
items.why_included: A commenter said it has been their default reader for years.
items.evidence: One user said they have used MoonReader for many years and tuned it for a comfortable phone-and-tablet reading setup.

items.name: Mir Books
items.source_story_title: Mir Books – Books from the Soviet Era
items.summary: A catalog of Soviet-era books and technical translations.
items.why_included: The thread drew strong nostalgia and direct archive links.
items.evidence: Commenters praised Mir Publishers' science and math books and linked archive copies and translations.
